description_long: This is a two-story timber-framed house with a steeply pitched gable roof covered in weathered yellowish-brown shingles. The front facade features two small, shuttered windows on the upper floor and a recessed entryway below, sheltered by a wooden overhang. The side walls display a half-timbered pattern with vertical and horizontal wooden beams framing white plaster panels, and one side includes a set of four narrow, vertically aligned windows. The rear of the house is plain, with no windows or doors, emphasizing the timber framing. The structure sits on a slightly raised base, and the overall design suggests a traditional European or medieval architectural influence. The materials and proportions indicate a sturdy, residential dwelling suitable for a small family.
description: A two-story, timber-framed house with a steep gable roof, shuttered windows, and a recessed entryway, featuring a traditional European design.
description_view_0: Front view showing two upper windows with shutters, a recessed entryway, and a wooden overhang.
description_view_1: Side view displaying four narrow vertical windows and prominent timber framing.
description_view_2: Rear view with plain white plaster panels and visible timber framing, no windows or doors.
description_view_3: Opposite side view similar to view 1, with four narrow windows and timber framing.
attribute_window_arrangement: symmetrical rows on front, vertical strips on sides
attribute_entryway_design: Recessed wooden door with overhang, no steps, simple and functional
